terms.definition: Penalty proportional to the sum of squared model weights (||w||₂²), added to the training loss to shrink weights and reduce overfitting; also called weight decay in neural networks.
Penalty proportional to the sum of absolute model weights (||w||₁), added to the training loss to encourage sparsity—some weights can become exactly zero.
Scalar λ (alpha in scikit-learn) that scales the penalty term Ω(θ) in L_total = L_data + λ Ω(θ); λ = 0 is unregularized, larger λ restricts the model more.
Neural-network name for L2 regularization on connection weights, typically applied in the optimizer alongside the data loss (e.g. Adam weight_decay).
Training objective combining data fit L_data(θ) with a complexity penalty λ Ω(θ); the canonical form for L1/L2 overfitting mitigation.
